compiled_state.title: Implement preconditions-depth-classifier.sh — 8-dim mapping to manifest_depth and schema_version
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## Context
Story 86ad-f5e7. Implements the spike classifier that maps complexity-evaluator classification output to manifest_depth and schema_version. Must make T1 (RED tests) pass. Depends on T1 (RED tests must exist first) and T6 (contract stub pins the schema).

**Spike flag**: This classifier is explicitly a spike with heuristic weights. The mapping (TRIVIAL→minimal, MODERATE→standard, COMPLEX→deep) is a starting heuristic. Story 6 auto-fallback (FP rate >10%) is the guard against misclassification in production.

## Implementation

Create: `plugins/dso/scripts/preconditions-depth-classifier.sh`

Set executable bit: `chmod +x plugins/dso/scripts/preconditions-depth-classifier.sh`

### Script contract

Sources or is sourced by callers that already have `_write_preconditions()` available. Exposes:

```bash
# _classify_manifest_depth <classification>
# Maps complexity-evaluator classification → (manifest_depth, schema_version)
# Outputs two lines to stdout:
#   manifest_depth=<minimal|standard|deep>
#   schema_version=<1|2>
# Falls back to minimal/1 on unknown classification (spike FP fallback)
# Exit 0 always (fail-open per spike policy)
```

### Mapping table (per contract doc T6)

| classification | manifest_depth | schema_version |
|----------------|---------------|----------------|
| TRIVIAL        | minimal        | 1              |
| SIMPLE         | minimal        | 1              |
| MODERATE       | standard       | 2              |
| COMPLEX        | deep           | 2              |
| (unknown)      | minimal        | 1              |

### Extended field definitions (schema_version=2)

When invoked with depth=standard, `_write_preconditions()` payload SHOULD include these additional keys (empty arrays are valid):
- `extended_gate_verdicts`: array of extended gate verdict objects (beyond the minimal gate_verdicts composite)
- `derived_invariants`: array of invariant strings derived from the stage context

When invoked with depth=deep, payload SHOULD additionally include:
- `full_trace`: array of stage execution trace entries
- `dependency_chain`: array of dependency ticket IDs forming the upstream chain

These are field-set definitions; the classifier script itself does not write the payload — it returns the (manifest_depth, schema_version) pair. The caller constructs the payload.

### Writer integration

After classification, caller pattern:
```bash
# In brainstorm writer or sprint:
source "${_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/preconditions-depth-classifier.sh"
eval "$(_classify_manifest_depth "${CLASSIFICATION}")"
# Now manifest_depth and schema_version are in environment
_write_preconditions "${TICKET_ID}" "${STAGE}" "${manifest_depth}" "${payload}"
```

## File Impact

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `plugins/dso/scripts/preconditions-depth-classifier.sh` | Create |
| `tests/scripts/test-preconditions-depth-classifier.sh` | Tests pass (T1) |

## Consumer Detection

No external consumers yet (Story 1 brainstorm writer will be updated to call this in a follow-on; validators in Story 2/4 will also call it). This is a new script — no breaking changes.

## Pattern Reference
# from plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib.sh: _write_preconditions pattern (Story 1 establishes this)
# General pattern for sourced bash functions with stdout output:
# function outputs key=value pairs; caller uses eval "$(...)" to bring into env

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Script exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/preconditions-depth-classifier.sh
- [ ] All 8 RED tests in T1 pass
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-preconditions-depth-classifier.sh 2>&1 | grep -q "FAILED: 0"
- [ ] TRIVIAL maps to minimal with schema_version=1
  Verify: bash -c 'source $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/preconditions-depth-classifier.sh; eval "$(_classify_manifest_depth TRIVIAL)"; test "$manifest_depth" = "minimal" && test "$schema_version" = "1"'
- [ ] COMPLEX maps to deep with schema_version=2
  Verify: bash -c 'source $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/preconditions-depth-classifier.sh; eval "$(_classify_manifest_depth COMPLEX)"; test "$manifest_depth" = "deep" && test "$schema_version" = "2"'
- [ ] Unknown classification falls back to minimal (fail-open spike policy)
  Verify: bash -c 'source $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/preconditions-depth-classifier.sh; eval "$(_classify_manifest_depth UNKNOWN)"; test "$manifest_depth" = "minimal"'
- [ ] check-plugin-self-ref.sh passes
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/hooks/pre-commit/check-plugin-self-ref.sh
- [ ] check-portability.sh passes
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/hooks/pre-commit/check-portability.sh

## Retry Budget
MAX_ATTEMPTS: 3 (sonnet model)
On 3 consecutive sonnet failures: escalate to opus with full diagnostic context (all 3 failure messages)
On 3 consecutive opus failures (6 total): escalate to user with full failure history
If MAX_AGENTS: 0 at sonnet→opus escalation time: skip opus step, escalate to user immediately
